headline: The Standings Are Lying to You, and Other Week 20 Fever Dreams
subheadline: BaseballPro77 delivers a beatdown for the ages, the Skidmarks get edged out by marsupials, and our standings page apparently took a week off.
body:
Look, I don't know who broke the standings computer, but right now we're looking at a ten-way tie at 0-0 heading into the final stretch of the regular season. It's giving major Westworld Season 2 energy—nothing makes sense, the timeline is cooked, and I'm pretty sure someone in accounting is a robot.

But the GAMES? The games still happened, baby. And they were *fascinating*.

**BaseballPro77** absolutely bodied **General Hospital** this week, 9.0–1.0, in what can only be described as a televised crime. This was the fantasy equivalent of that scene in Casino where Joe Pesci just... you know what, I won't finish that thought. Let's just say General Hospital needed a crash cart and didn't get one. BaseballPro77 is the team nobody wants to see in their bracket come playoff time—the kind of squad that makes you check your lineup six times on Tuesday afternoon because you KNOW they're coming.

(Side note: naming your team BaseballPro77 is either incredibly lazy or a galaxy-brain move to make opponents underestimate you. I genuinely can't decide which.)

Meanwhile, the **Canberra Wombats** squeaked past the **Buttville Skidmarks** 5.0–4.0 in what I'm contractually obligated to call a "nail-biter between two unfortunately named franchises." The Wombats are one of those sneaky-good teams that keep winning 5-4 games while you're not paying attention—very 2011 Cardinals energy. The Skidmarks? They're the guy who shows up to your poker night, loses by $3, and spends the whole drive home explaining how he ACTUALLY won.

The **Athletic Park Dragons** blitzed the **Albuquerque Bombers** 7.0–2.0, which... Bombers, what happened? That's not even competitive. That's bringing a vuvuzela to a knife fight.

And shoutout to the **Hiroshima Radiation** and **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** for fighting to a 4.0–4.0 draw, which is the fantasy equivalent of two people reaching for the same armrest on a Southwest flight and just... leaving their elbows there. Uncomfortable for everyone.

As for the standings situation—I've been assured it'll be fixed by next week. (Narrator: It would not be fixed by next week.)

Buckle up. Playoff positioning is about to get WEIRD.
